prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a statistical model, specifically a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) which assumes stock prices follow a random walk with a drift. For WFC, estimated annual parameters include an average drift (mean return) of approximately 10% and an annual volatility of approximately 25%, typical for a large-cap financial stock, derived from historical market data. These annual parameters were converted to daily equivalents (daily drift = 0.0003968, daily volatility = 0.01575) and then extrapolated for a 6-day period using the square root of time rule for volatility and linear scaling for drift. The point estimate represents the expected price after 6 days, incorporating the average daily drift. The confidence intervals are calculated using the derived 6-day volatility and standard normal distribution Z-scores corresponding to the desired confidence levels (50%, 80%, 90%), considering the logarithmic nature of returns.
